title: rubus rosaceus subsp. powellii
intro: Rubus rosaceus subsp. powellii, also known as Powell's raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus rosaceus plant, which is native to the southeastern United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Rubus rosaceus subsp. powellii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
